response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong polyglot background, including DLang (low-latency, async I/O at Sociomantic) and C/C++ exposure at National Instruments, plus genuine GIS/geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role demands modern C++ or Rust as a primary skill, and the resume shows no recent C++ work (last C/C++ role ended 2015) and no Rust experience. Recent work is TypeScript/Node-heavy. Worth a screening call to probe depth of systems/C++ chops and willingness to ramp back into C++.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/navigation data experience (GISCollective, Open Green Map)
Low-latency, distributed systems background at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-perf DBs)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
concerns: No modern C++ experience in last ~10 years; no Rust at all
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node/AWS — far from a C++ routing pipeline
No explicit routing/tiling or navigation guidance QA experience

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ / Rust
